Let me go through the plan carefully again with fresh eyes. ## Issue 1: Board test 5 - loading state — the `render` is called after `mockReturnValue` but there's no render call shown For tests 5 and 6, the plan says: - "call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })`, render, check..." - "call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })`, check..." The real issue is that `beforeEach` already sets up `useBoard.mockReturnValue(DEFAULT_STATE)` before each test runs, so for tests 5-11 that need different mock states, I need to call `useBoard.mockReturnValue(...)` with the custom state inside the test itself, before rendering. The plan does specify this order correctly, but it's not explicit enough that the mock needs to be set up before the component renders, otherwise the component will use the default state from `beforeEach`. Now looking at the `getAllByRole('region')` check in Board.test.jsx test 1 - since Column.jsx uses `
`, those sections will properly have the `region` ARIA role. The test expecting 3 regions should work correctly as long as there are exactly 3 columns being rendered. For the Column test using `getByLabelText('3 cards')`, this will correctly target the `` element with that aria-label, not the section itself, so there's no collision issue there. Looking at test 8 for the Board component, the setup has the card only in the `in_progress` column with `ready` empty, so there's no duplication problem. But I need to check what test 7 is doing to make sure the test sequence makes sense. The real issue is that these tests only verify the card exists somewhere in the document, not that it's actually rendered in the correct column. I need to use more specific queries—like targeting the column region first—to ensure each test is validating the card appears in the right place, otherwise a card in the wrong column would still pass. The real issue is that tests 7, 8, and 9 all use the same card title but only place it in one column each, so they'd pass even if the Board incorrectly rendered cards in multiple columns—I need to wrap the assertions with `within()` to verify cards appear in the correct column specifically. I also notice the `within` function isn't imported from testing-library, so I'll need to add that to the imports. Looking at test 5, it checks for a loading indicator and verifies columns are absent when loading is true, but I should examine what that test actually expects. Board returns early with `
Loading…
`. There are no columns rendered. Checking columns are absent could be done with `expect(screen.queryAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(0)`. The plan doesn't spell this out - just "columns are absent". This is ambiguous for an LLM. For test 11, the "same setup" language is vague about needing to override the default mock with one that includes MOCK_CARD in the ready state. Without explicitly setting `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], ... } })`, an LLM might just render with the empty DEFAULT_STATE and have nothing to click. Test 5 is checking `screen.queryByText('Short desc')` when the card is rendered with `description: null`, but that text only exists if description actually contains 'Short desc' — the test needs to verify the description element itself isn't rendered, not check for specific text that was never there. the description text doesn't appear when it's null, which is what we're testing for. That works fine. Now looking at the CardTile test for description truncation - the task mentions displaying a truncated description, and the CSS uses `-webkit-line-clamp: 2` to handle it. But the tests don't actually verify that the truncation class gets applied. Since CSS isn't computed in jsdom, I can't fully test the truncation behavior there anyway. The test confirms the text renders when provided, and the CSS is in place, so this is acceptable. I'm noticing an issue with the Board test file - it's defining `DEFAULT_STATE` at module level with `vi.fn()` calls... Actually, these mock functions are created once and stay the same across all tests. If a test calls `vi.clearAllMocks()`, those module-level functions won't reset unless I explicitly reset them. But since the Board component only destructures `cards`, `loading`, and `error` from `useBoard()` and never touches the mutation functions, this doesn't cause any real problems. Moving the `vi.fn()` calls into `beforeEach` would be cleaner, but it's not a correctness issue. For the close button selector in Board test 11, I need to be careful - once the modal opens, there are now two elements with close-related labels: the CardTile itself has `aria-label="Test Card"` and the modal's close button has `aria-label="Close"`. The selector `getByRole('button', { name: /close/i })` should target the modal's close button specifically, which is what I want. In CardModal test 9, I'm using `document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')` to click the overlay. While this works fine since testing-library renders components into the actual DOM, it would be more idiomatic to destructure `container` from the render call and use `container.querySelector` instead. It's not a blocking issue, but the plan should note this preference. For the Board tests that need `within()` assertions to verify cards appear in the correct column, I'll need to add `within` to the imports from `@testing-library/react`. Looking at the critical issues to address: Board tests 7-9 need to scope their card queries to specific columns using `within()`, tests 8-9 have ambiguous "same but" descriptions that should spell out the full mock state, test 11's "same setup" reference needs to be explicit about calling `useBoard.mockReturnValue()` again, and test 5's "columns are absent" language is too vague and needs clarification. The real problem with tests 7-9 is that `screen.getByText('Test Card')` passes regardless of which column the card ends up in—it just checks that the card exists somewhere in the DOM. Since the requirement is to verify cards appear in the correct columns, I need to use `within()` to scope the query to each specific column region, which would catch cases where the Board incorrectly passes the wrong array to the wrong column. There's also an ambiguity in how tests 8 and 9 should set up their mocks. When I say "same but with in_progress", an LLM could interpret that as spreading test 7's full card state and just overriding in_progress, which would leave the ready column populated. I need to be explicit that each test should have only one column populated with its respective card. I'm also noticing that DEFAULT_STATE has vi.fn() calls at the module level, and while these won't reset between tests, it's not a problem since Board only uses cards, loading, and error—the mock functions are never invoked by the component. One last concern: the position field in makeCard is being set to the id string rather than a number, which could cause sorting issues downstream in groupCards, though since we're mocking useBoard directly in these tests, that's not a blocker here. The real issue is that card IDs should be UUIDs (strings) while position needs to be numeric, so I should refactor makeCard to accept position as a separate parameter instead of deriving it from the id. Now I'm identifying the specific test fixes needed: updating Board tests 7-9 to query regions by name and use within() for assertions, making test 5's absence check explicit, ensuring test 11 repeats its mock setup rather than referencing prior state, and correcting the Column fixture's position field to be numeric.

content.text:
I found four correctness issues. Let me fix them:

**Issue 1 — Board tests 7–9 don't verify cards are in the *correct* column.** `screen.getByText('Test Card')` would pass even if the Board renders every card in the wrong column. The requirement is "verify cards appear in correct columns", so assertions must be scoped with `within()`.

**Issue 2 — Board tests 8–9 "same but…" phrasing is ambiguous.** An LLM could spread the ready array from test 7, creating duplicate cards across columns. Full mock state must be spelled out.

**Issue 3 — Board test 5 "columns are absent" is vague.** Needs an explicit assertion.

**Issue 4 — Board test 11 "same setup" is ambiguous.** The `beforeEach` resets to `DEFAULT_STATE` (no cards), so "same setup" is not enough — the mock must be explicitly reconfigured before rendering.

**Bonus — Column fixture has `position: id` (string).** `id` is a string like `'1'`, so `position` becomes the string `'1'` instead of a number, which mismatches the Card type. Minor but incorrect mock data.

# Plan: Create Board, Column, and CardTile Components (Task 10) ## Context The Kanban board app has a working API client (`client/src/api/client.js`) and a `useBoard` custom hook (`client/src/hooks/useBoard.js`) that manages grouped card state. `App.jsx` is a placeholder with a comment where the Board should go. No UI components exist yet. This task builds the visual layer: Board → Column → CardTile, plus a basic CardModal activated by clicking a card. --- ## Architecture Decisions - **Plain CSS files** (e.g. `Board.css`) — consistent with `App.css`; no CSS modules or styled-components - **Flexbox** for the three-column layout (no CSS Grid needed here) - **`useBoard()` called in `Board.jsx`** — column/card data flows down as props; Column and CardTile are pure presentational - **`selectedCard` state in `Board.jsx`** — drives modal visibility; passed down via `onCardClick` prop chain - **All component files co-located** under `client/src/components/Board/` - **Testing**: Vitest + @testing-library/react, matching existing patterns; `vi.mock` to isolate `useBoard` --- ## Files to Create | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` | Board tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Board container | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.css` | Board styles | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` | Column tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` | Column component | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.css` | Column styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx` | CardTile tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx` | CardTile component | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.css` | CardTile styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | CardModal tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Card detail modal | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css` | Modal overlay styles | ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/App.jsx` | Import and render `` in `
` | | `client/src/App.test.jsx` | Add `vi.mock` for `useBoard` (Board now renders inside App) | --- ## Reusable Existing Code - **`useBoard`** at `client/src/hooks/useBoard.js` — provides `{ cards, loading, error }` where `cards = { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }` - **`columnToKey`** exported from `useBoard.js` — maps `'in-progress'` → `'in_progress'` - **Card shape**: `{ id, title, assignee: string|null, description: string|null, column, position, created_at, comments[] }` - **Test mock pattern** from `useBoard.test.js`: `vi.mock('../api/client.js', () => ({ fetchCards: vi.fn() }))` and `mockResponse()` helper --- ## TDD Implementation Plan ### Subtask 1 — Board Layout Component #### 1a. Write failing tests: `Board.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Board from './Board.jsx' vi.mock('../../hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn() })) const MOCK_CARD = { id: 'c1', title: 'Test Card', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'A description', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } const DEFAULT_STATE = { cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), } beforeEach(() => { useBoard.mockReturnValue(DEFAULT_STATE) }) ``` Test cases: 1. **"renders three column regions"** — `expect(screen.getAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(3)` 2. **"renders Ready column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` 3. **"renders In Progress column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'In Progress' })` 4. **"renders Done column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Done' })` 5. **"shows loading indicator when loading is true"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })`, render, check `screen.getByLabelText('Loading')` is present and columns are absent 6. **"shows error banner when error is set"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })`, check `screen.getByRole('alert')` contains `'fetch failed'` 7. **"passes ready cards to Ready column"** — `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })`, render, verify `screen.getByText('Test Card')` is in the document 8. **"passes in_progress cards to In Progress column"** — same but `in_progress: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, column: 'in-progress' }]` 9. **"passes done cards to Done column"** — same but `done: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, column: 'done' }]` 10. **"opens modal when a card is clicked"** — `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })`, render, Preserve all 4 existing tests unchanged: ```js import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, vi } from 'vitest' import App from './App.jsx' vi.mock('./hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn(() => ({ cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), })) })) // ... existing 4 test cases remain unchanged ``` Note: `vi.mock` is hoisted by Vitest automatically, so placement in the file is safe. The mock path `'./hooks/useBoard.js'` is relative to `App.test.jsx` (`client/src/`) and resolves to the same module that Board.jsx imports via `'../../hooks/useBoard.js'` — Vitest deduplicates by resolved path. --- ## TDD Execution Order (per subtask) For each subtask: 1. **Red**: Create the `*.test.jsx` file, run `npm test -- --run` → confirm failures 2. **Green**: Create the `*.jsx` and `*.css` files → run tests → confirm pass 3. **Refactor**: Clean up while keeping tests green Run tests from `client/` directory: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test -- --run ``` Or watch mode during development: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test ``` --- ## Verification Checklist - [ ] All new test files pass: Board, Column, CardTile, CardModal - [ ] Existing tests still pass: App, useBoard, api/client - [ ] `npm run build` completes without errors - [ ] Dev server (`npm run dev`) shows three-column board at localhost:5173 - [ ] Cards from the API appear in the correct column - [ ] Clicking a card opens the modal with title, assignee, description - [ ] Pressing Escape or clicking overlay closes the modal - [ ] Responsive: columns stack on narrow screens - [ ] Keyboard navigation: Tab → card, Enter/Space → opens modal

# Plan: Create Board, Column, and CardTile Components (Task 10) ## Context The Kanban board app has a working API client (`client/src/api/client.js`) and a `useBoard` custom hook (`client/src/hooks/useBoard.js`) that manages grouped card state. `App.jsx` is a placeholder with a comment where the Board should go. No UI components exist yet. This task builds the visual layer: Board → Column → CardTile, plus a basic CardModal activated by clicking a card. --- ## Architecture Decisions - **Plain CSS files** (e.g. `Board.css`) — consistent with `App.css`; no CSS modules or styled-components - **Flexbox** for the three-column layout (no CSS Grid needed here) - **`useBoard()` called in `Board.jsx`** — column/card data flows down as props; Column and CardTile are pure presentational - **`selectedCard` state in `Board.jsx`** — drives modal visibility; passed down via `onCardClick` prop chain - **All component files co-located** under `client/src/components/Board/` - **Testing**: Vitest + @testing-library/react, matching existing patterns; `vi.mock` to isolate `useBoard` --- ## Files to Create | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` | Board tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Board container | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.css` | Board styles | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` | Column tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` | Column component | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.css` | Column styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx` | CardTile tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx` | CardTile component | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.css` | CardTile styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | CardModal tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Card detail modal | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css` | Modal overlay styles | ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/App.jsx` | Import and render `` in `
` | | `client/src/App.test.jsx` | Add `vi.mock` for `useBoard` (Board now renders inside App) | --- ## Reusable Existing Code - **`useBoard`** at `client/src/hooks/useBoard.js` — provides `{ cards, loading, error }` where `cards = { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }` - **`columnToKey`** exported from `useBoard.js` — maps `'in-progress'` → `'in_progress'` - **Card shape**: `{ id, title, assignee: string|null, description: string|null, column, position, created_at, comments[] }` - **Test mock pattern** from `useBoard.test.js`: `vi.mock('../api/client.js', () => ({ fetchCards: vi.fn() }))` and `mockResponse()` helper --- ## TDD Implementation Plan ### Subtask 1 — Board Layout Component #### 1a. Write failing tests: `Board.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent, within } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Board from './Board.jsx' vi.mock('../../hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn() })) const MOCK_CARD = { id: 'c1', title: 'Test Card', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'A description', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } const DEFAULT_STATE = { cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), } beforeEach(() => { useBoard.mockReturnValue(DEFAULT_STATE) }) ``` Test cases: 1. **"renders three column regions"** — `expect(screen.getAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(3)` 2. **"renders Ready column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` 3. **"renders In Progress column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'In Progress' })` 4. **"renders Done column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Done' })` 5. **"shows loading indicator when loading is true"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })`, render, check `screen.getByLabelText('Loading')` is present and columns are absent 6. **"shows error banner when error is set"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })`, check `screen.getByRole('alert')` contains `'fetch failed'` 7. **"passes ready cards to Ready column"** — `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })`, render, verify `screen.getByText('Test Card')` is in the document 8. **"passes in_progress cards to In Progress column"** — same but `in_progress: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, column: 'in-progress' }]` 9. **"passes done cards to Done column"** — same but `done: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, column: 'done' }]` 10. **"opens modal when a card is clicked"** — `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })`, render, `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`, verify `screen.getByRole('dialog')` appears 11. **"closes modal when close button is clicked"** — same setup, open modal, then `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))`, verify `screen.queryByRole('dialog')` is null #### 1b. Implement `Board.jsx` ```jsx import { useState } from 'react' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Column from './Column.jsx' import CardModal from './CardModal.jsx' import './Board.css' export default function Board() { const { cards, loading, error } = useBoard() const [selectedCard, setSelectedCard] = useState(null) if (loading) return
Loading…
if (error) return
{error}
return (
{selectedCard && setSelectedCard(null)} />}
) } ``` #### 1c. `Board.css` ```css .board { display: flex; gap: 1rem; align-items: flex-start; } .board-loading { text-align: center; padding: 2rem; color: #666; } .board-error { padding: 1rem; background: #fee; color: #c00; border-radius: 4px; } @media (max-width: 768px) { .board { flex-direction: column; } } ``` --- ### Subtask 2 — Column Component #### 2a. Write failing tests: `Column.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest' import Column from './Column.jsx' const makeCard = (id, title) => ({ id, title, assignee: 'Alice', description: 'desc', column: 'ready', position: id, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], }) const MOCK_CARDS = [makeCard('1', 'Card One'), makeCard('2', 'Card Two'), makeCard('3', 'Card Three')] ``` Test cases — render with ``: 1. **"renders the column title"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` 2. **"renders card count equal to number of cards"** — badge span has text content `'3'`; query with `screen.getByLabelText('3 cards')` (the `aria-label` on the count span) 3. **"renders 0 count for empty column"** — render with `cards={[]}`, `screen.getByLabelText('0 cards')` 4. **"renders a CardTile for each card"** — `screen.getByText('Card One')`, `screen.getByText('Card Two')`, `screen.getByText('Card Three')` all present 5. **"renders empty state message when no cards"** — render with `cards={[]}`, `screen.getByText(/no cards/i)` 6. **"calls onCardClick with the correct card when a CardTile is clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, click `screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Card One' })`, verify `handler` called with `MOCK_CARDS[0]` #### 2b. Implement `Column.jsx` ```jsx import CardTile from './CardTile.jsx' import './Column.css' export default function Column({ title, cards, onCardClick }) { return (

{title}

{cards.length}
{cards.length === 0 ?

No cards

: cards.map(card => ( )) }
) } ``` #### 2c. `Column.css` ```css .column { flex: 1; min-width: 240px; background: #e8eaf0; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.75rem; } .column-header { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; } .column-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1a1a2e; } .column-count { background: #1a1a2e; color: #fff; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.1rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.8rem; } .column-cards { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; } .column-empty { color: #888; font-size: 0.875rem; text-align: center; padding: 1rem 0; } ``` --- ### Subtask 3 — CardTile Component #### 3a. Write failing tests: `CardTile.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest' import CardTile from './CardTile.jsx' const card = { id: '1', title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'Short desc', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } ``` All tests render with `render()` unless noted. Test cases: 1. **"renders card title"** — `screen.getByText('Fix bug')` 2. **"renders assignee name when provided"** — `screen.getByText('Alice')` 3. **"renders 'Unassigned' when assignee is null"** — render with `{ ...card, assignee: null }`, check `screen.getByText('Unassigned')` 4. **"renders description when provided"** — `screen.getByText('Short desc')` 5. **"does not render description element when description is null"** — render with `{ ...card, description: null }`, verify `screen.queryByText('Short desc')` is null 6. **"calls onCardClick with the card object when clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Fix bug' }))`, verify `handler` called with `card` 7. **"calls onCardClick when Enter key is pressed"** — `fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('button'), { key: 'Enter' })`, verify handler called with `card` 8. **"calls onCardClick when Space key is pressed"** — `fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('button'), { key: ' ' })`, verify handler called with `card` 9. **"is keyboard-focusable"** — `expect(screen.getByRole('button')).toHaveAttribute('tabindex', '0')` #### 3b. Implement `CardTile.jsx` ```jsx import './CardTile.css' export default function CardTile({ card, onCardClick }) { function handleKeyDown(e) { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault() onCardClick(card) } } return (
onCardClick(card)} onKeyDown={handleKeyDown} aria-label={card.title} >

{card.title}

{card.assignee ?? 'Unassigned'}

{card.description && (

{card.description}

)}
) } ``` #### 3c. `CardTile.css` ```css .card-tile { background: #fff; border-radius: 6px; padding: 0.75rem; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,.1); transition: box-shadow 0.15s, transform 0.15s; } .card-tile:hover, .card-tile:focus { box-shadow: 0 3px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.15); transform: translateY(-1px); outline: 2px solid #4a6fa5; } .card-tile-title { font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1a1a2e; margin-bottom: 0.25rem; } .card-tile-assignee { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #666; margin-bottom: 0.25rem; } .card-tile-description { font-size: 0.8rem; color: #444; overflow: hidden; display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; } ``` --- ### Subtask 4 — CardModal + CSS Styling #### 4a. Write failing tests: `CardModal.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest' import CardModal from './CardModal.jsx' const card = { id: '1', title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'Some description', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } ``` All tests render with `render()` unless noted. Test cases: 1. **"has dialog role"** — `screen.getByRole('dialog')` is in the document 2. **"renders card title inside dialog"** — `screen.getByRole('dialog')` contains text `'Fix bug'` 3. **"renders assignee name"** — dialog contains text `'Alice'` 4. **"renders 'Unassigned' when assignee is null"** — render with `{ ...card, assignee: null }`, dialog contains `'Unassigned'` 5. **"renders description when present"** — dialog contains `'Some description'` 6. **"renders 'No description' when description is null"** — render with `{ ...card, description: null }`, dialog contains `'No description'` 7. **"calls onClose when close button is clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, render with `onClose={handler}`, `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))`, verify `handler` called once 8. **"calls onClose when Escape key is pressed"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, render with `onClose={handler}`, `fireEvent.keyDown(document.body, { key: 'Escape' })`, verify `handler` called once 9. **"calls onClose when modal overlay is clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, render with `onClose={handler}`, `fireEvent.click(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))`, verify `handler` called once 10. **"does not call onClose when modal content area is clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, render with `onClose={handler}`, `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('dialog'))`, verify `handler` NOT called #### 4b. Implement `CardModal.jsx` ```jsx import { useEffect } from 'react' import './CardModal.css' export default function CardModal({ card, onClose }) { useEffect(() => { function onKey(e) { if (e.key === 'Escape') onClose() } document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) }, [onClose]) return (
e.stopPropagation()}>

{card.title}

Assignee: {card.assignee ?? 'Unassigned'}

{card.description ?? 'No description'}

) } ``` #### 4c. `CardModal.css` ```css .modal-overlay { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0,0,0,.5); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; z-index: 100; } .modal-content { background: #fff; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.5rem; max-width: 480px; width: 90%; position: relative; box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,.2); } .modal-close { position: absolute; top: 0.75rem; right: 0.75rem; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.1rem; color: #666; } .modal-close:hover { color: #1a1a2e; } .modal-title { font-size: 1.2rem; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; color: #1a1a2e; } .modal-assignee { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #555; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; } .modal-description { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #333; line-height: 1.5; } ``` --- ### App.jsx Update Modify `client/src/App.jsx`: ```jsx import Board from './components/Board/Board.jsx' import './App.css' function App() { return (

Kanban Board

) } export default App ``` Modify `client/src/App.test.jsx` — add `vi.mock` for `useBoard` at the top (before imports) since Board now renders inside App and would otherwise trigger real API calls. Preserve all 4 existing tests unchanged: ```js import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, vi } from 'vitest' import App from './App.jsx' vi.mock('./hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn(() => ({ cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), })) })) // ... existing 4 test cases remain unchanged ``` Note: `vi.mock` is hoisted by Vitest automatically, so placement in the file is safe. -8. **"passes in_progress cards to In Progress column"** — same but `in_progress: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, column: 'in-progress' }]`
-9. **"passes done cards to Done column"** — same but `done: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, column: 'done' }]`

-10. **"opens modal when a card is clicked"** — `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })`, render, `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`, verify `screen.getByRole('dialog')` appears
-11. **"closes modal when close button is clicked"** — same setup, open modal, then `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))`, verify `screen.queryByRole('dialog')` is null

+5. **"shows loading indicator when loading is true"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })` then render; assert `screen.getByLabelText('Loading')` is in the document AND `expect(screen.queryAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(0)` (no columns rendered)

+6. **"shows error banner when error is set"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })` then render; assert `screen.getByRole('alert')` contains text `'fetch failed'`

+7. **"passes ready cards to Ready column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Ready' })).getByText('Test Card')` — verifies card is inside the Ready column specifically

+8. **"passes in_progress cards to In Progress column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c2', column: 'in-progress' }], done: [] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'In Progress' })).getByText('Test Card')`

+9. **"passes done cards to Done column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c3', column: 'done' }] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Done' })).getByText('Test Card')`

+10. **"opens modal when a card is clicked"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`; verify `screen.getByRole('dialog')` appears

+11. **"closes modal when close button is clicked"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; open modal by `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`; then `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))`; verify `screen.queryByRole('dialog')` is null

# Plan: Create Board, Column, and CardTile Components (Task 10) ## Context The Kanban board app has a working API client (`client/src/api/client.js`) and a `useBoard` custom hook (`client/src/hooks/useBoard.js`) that manages grouped card state. `App.jsx` is a placeholder with a comment where the Board should go. No UI components exist yet. This task builds the visual layer: Board → Column → CardTile, plus a basic CardModal activated by clicking a card. --- ## Architecture Decisions - **Plain CSS files** (e.g. `Board.css`) — consistent with `App.css`; no CSS modules or styled-components - **Flexbox** for the three-column layout (no CSS Grid needed here) - **`useBoard()` called in `Board.jsx`** — column/card data flows down as props; Column and CardTile are pure presentational - **`selectedCard` state in `Board.jsx`** — drives modal visibility; passed down via `onCardClick` prop chain - **All component files co-located** under `client/src/components/Board/` - **Testing**: Vitest + @testing-library/react, matching existing patterns; `vi.mock` to isolate `useBoard` --- ## Files to Create | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` | Board tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Board container | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.css` | Board styles | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` | Column tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` | Column component | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.css` | Column styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx` | CardTile tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx` | CardTile component | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.css` | CardTile styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | CardModal tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Card detail modal | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css` | Modal overlay styles | ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/App.jsx` | Import and render `` in `
` | | `client/src/App.test.jsx` | Add `vi.mock` for `useBoard` (Board now renders inside App) | --- ## Reusable Existing Code - **`useBoard`** at `client/src/hooks/useBoard.js` — provides `{ cards, loading, error }` where `cards = { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }` - **`columnToKey`** exported from `useBoard.js` — maps `'in-progress'` → `'in_progress'` - **Card shape**: `{ id, title, assignee: string|null, description: string|null, column, position, created_at, comments[] }` - **Test mock pattern** from `useBoard.test.js`: `vi.mock('../api/client.js', () => ({ fetchCards: vi.fn() }))` and `mockResponse()` helper --- ## TDD Implementation Plan ### Subtask 1 — Board Layout Component #### 1a. Write failing tests: `Board.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent, within } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Board from './Board.jsx' vi.mock('../../hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn() })) const MOCK_CARD = { id: 'c1', title: 'Test Card', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'A description', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } const DEFAULT_STATE = { cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), } beforeEach(() => { useBoard.mockReturnValue(DEFAULT_STATE) }) ``` Test cases: 1. **"renders three column regions"** — `expect(screen.getAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(3)` 2. **"renders Ready column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` 3. **"renders In Progress column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'In Progress' })` 4. **"renders Done column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Done' })` 5. **"shows loading indicator when loading is true"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })` then render; assert `screen.getByLabelText('Loading')` is in the document AND `expect(screen.queryAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(0)` (no columns rendered) 6. **"shows error banner when error is set"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })` then render; assert `screen.getByRole('alert')` contains text `'fetch failed'` 7. **"passes ready cards to Ready column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Ready' })).getByText('Test Card')` — verifies card is inside the Ready column specifically 8. **"passes in_progress cards to In Progress column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c2', column: 'in-progress' }], done: [] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'In Progress' })).getByText('Test Card')` 9. **"passes done cards to Done column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c3', column: 'done' }] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Done' })).getByText('Test Card')` 10. **"opens modal when a card is clicked"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`; verify `screen.getByRole('dialog')` appears 11. **"closes modal when close button is clicked"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; open modal by `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`; then `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))`; verify `screen.queryByRole('dialog')` is null #### 1b. Kanban Board

) } export default App ``` Modify `client/src/App.test.jsx` — add `vi.mock` for `useBoard` at the top (before imports) since Board now renders inside App and would otherwise trigger real API calls. Preserve all 4 existing tests unchanged: ```js import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, vi } from 'vitest' import App from './App.jsx' vi.mock('./hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn(() => ({ cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), })) })) // ... existing 4 test cases remain unchanged ``` Note: `vi.mock` is hoisted by Vitest automatically, so placement in the file is safe. The mock path `'./hooks/useBoard.js'` is relative to `App.test.jsx` (`client/src/`) and resolves to the same module that Board.jsx imports via `'../../hooks/useBoard.js'` — Vitest deduplicates by resolved path. --- ## TDD Execution Order (per subtask) For each subtask: 1. **Red**: Create the `*.test.jsx` file, run `npm test -- --run` → confirm failures 2. **Green**: Create the `*.jsx` and `*.css` files → run tests → confirm pass 3. **Refactor**: Clean up while keeping tests green Run tests from `client/` directory: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test -- --run ``` Or watch mode during development: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test ``` --- ## Verification Checklist - [ ] All new test files pass: Board, Column, CardTile, CardModal - [ ] Existing tests still pass: App, useBoard, api/client - [ ] `npm run build` completes without errors - [ ] Dev server (`npm run dev`) shows three-column board at localhost:5173 - [ ] Cards from the API appear in the correct column - [ ] Clicking a card opens the modal with title, assignee, description - [ ] Pressing Escape or clicking overlay closes the modal - [ ] Responsive: columns stack on narrow screens - [ ] Keyboard navigation: Tab → card, Enter/Space → opens modal

## TDD Implementation Plan ### Subtask 1 — Board Layout Component #### 1a. Write failing tests: `Board.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent, within } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Board from './Board.jsx' vi.mock('../../hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn() })) const MOCK_CARD = { id: 'c1', title: 'Test Card', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'A description', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } const DEFAULT_STATE = { cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), } beforeEach(() => { useBoard.mockReturnValue(DEFAULT_STATE) }) ``` Test cases: 1. **"renders three column regions"** — `expect(screen.getAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(3)` 2. **"renders Ready column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` 3. **"renders In Progress column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'In Progress' })` 4. **"renders Done column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Done' })` 5. **"shows loading indicator when loading is true"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })` then render; assert `screen.getByLabelText('Loading')` is in the document AND `expect(screen.queryAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(0)` (no columns rendered) 6. **"shows error banner when error is set"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })` then render; assert `screen.getByRole('alert')` contains text `'fetch failed'` 7. **"passes ready cards to Ready column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Ready' })).getByText('Test Card')` — verifies card is inside the Ready column specifically 8. **"passes in_progress cards to In Progress column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c2', column: 'in-progress' }], done: [] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'In Progress' })).getByText('Test Card')` 9. **"passes done cards to Done column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c3', column: 'done' }] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Done' })).getByText('Test Card')` 10. **"opens modal when a card is clicked"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`; verify `screen.getByRole('dialog')` appears 11. **"closes modal when close button is clicked"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; open modal by `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`; then `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))`; verify `screen.queryByRole('dialog')` is null #### 1b. Implement `Board.jsx` ```jsx import { useState } from 'react' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Column from './Column.jsx' import CardModal from './CardModal.jsx' import './Board.css' export default function Board() { const { cards, loading, error } = useBoard() const [selectedCard, setSelectedCard] = useState(null) if (loading) return

The plan looks correct and complete now. Here's a summary of the four fixes made:

1. **`within` import added** to `Board.test.jsx` imports (`render, screen, fireEvent, within`).

2. **Board tests 7–9 now use column-scoped assertions** via `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Ready' })).getByText('Test Card')` — this correctly verifies the card is in the *right* column, not just anywhere in the DOM.

3. **Board tests 8–9 fully spelled out** with explicit mock state (`ready: [], in_progress: [...], done: []`) instead of the ambiguous "same but…" phrasing, and use distinct card `id`s (`c2`, `c3`) to avoid duplicate-key issues.

4. **Board test 11** now explicitly repeats the full `useBoard.mockReturnValue(...)` call before rendering, rather than relying on the ambiguous "same setup" shorthand, so the `beforeEach` reset doesn't leave the component with no cards to click.

5. **Column fixture `makeCard` corrected**: `position` is now a proper numeric argument (`pos`) instead of reusing the string `id`, matching the actual Card type.

The mock path `'./hooks/useBoard.js'` is relative to `App.test.jsx` (`client/src/`) and resolves to the same module that Board.jsx imports via `'../../hooks/useBoard.js'` — Vitest deduplicates by resolved path. 396 397 --- 398 399 ## TDD Execution Order (per subtask) 400 401 For each subtask: 402 1. **Red**: Create the `*.test.jsx` file, run `npm test -- --run` → confirm failures 403 2. **Green**: Create the `*.jsx` and `*.css` files → run tests → confirm pass 404 3. **Refactor**: Clean up while keeping tests green 405 406 Run tests from `client/` directory: 407 ```bash 408 cd kanban/client && npm test -- --run 409 ``` 410 411 Or watch mode during development: 412 ```bash 413 cd kanban/client && npm test 414 ``` 415 416 --- 417 418 ## Verification Checklist 419 420 - [ ] All new test files pass: Board, Column, CardTile, CardModal 421 - [ ] Existing tests still pass: App, useBoard, api/client 422 - [ ] `npm run build` completes without errors 423 - [ ] Dev server (`npm run dev`) shows three-column board at localhost:5173 424 - [ ] Cards from the API appear in the correct column 425 - [ ] Clicking a card opens the modal with title, assignee, description 426 - [ ] Pressing Escape or clicking overlay closes the modal 427 - [ ] Responsive: columns stack on narrow screens 428 - [ ] Keyboard navigation: Tab → card, Enter/Space → opens modal 429

# Plan: Create Board, Column, and CardTile Components (Task 10) ## Context The Kanban board app has a working API client (`client/src/api/client.js`) and a `useBoard` custom hook (`client/src/hooks/useBoard.js`) that manages grouped card state. `App.jsx` is a placeholder with a comment where the Board should go. No UI components exist yet. This task builds the visual layer: Board → Column → CardTile, plus a basic CardModal activated by clicking a card. --- ## Architecture Decisions - **Plain CSS files** (e.g. `Board.css`) — consistent with `App.css`; no CSS modules or styled-components - **Flexbox** for the three-column layout (no CSS Grid needed here) - **`useBoard()` called in `Board.jsx`** — column/card data flows down as props; Column and CardTile are pure presentational - **`selectedCard` state in `Board.jsx`** — drives modal visibility; passed down via `onCardClick` prop chain - **All component files co-located** under `client/src/components/Board/` - **Testing**: Vitest + @testing-library/react, matching existing patterns; `vi.mock` to isolate `useBoard` --- ## Files to Create | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` | Board tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Board container | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.css` | Board styles | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` | Column tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` | Column component | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.css` | Column styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx` | CardTile tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx` | CardTile component | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.css` | CardTile styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | CardModal tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Card detail modal | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css` | Modal overlay styles | ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/App.jsx` | Import and render `` in `
` | | `client/src/App.test.jsx` | Add `vi.mock` for `useBoard` (Board now renders inside App) | --- ## Reusable Existing Code - **`useBoard`** at `client/src/hooks/useBoard.js` — provides `{ cards, loading, error }` where `cards = { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }` - **`columnToKey`** exported from `useBoard.js` — maps `'in-progress'` → `'in_progress'` - **Card shape**: `{ id, title, assignee: string|null, description: string|null, column, position, created_at, comments[] }` - **Test mock pattern** from `useBoard.test.js`: `vi.mock('../api/client.js', () => ({ fetchCards: vi.fn() }))` and `mockResponse()` helper --- ## TDD Implementation Plan ### Subtask 1 — Board Layout Component #### 1a. Write failing tests: `Board.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent, within } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Board from './Board.jsx' vi.mock('../../hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn() })) const MOCK_CARD = { id: 'c1', title: 'Test Card', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'A description', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } const DEFAULT_STATE = { cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), } beforeEach(() => { useBoard.mockReturnValue(DEFAULT_STATE) }) ``` Test cases: 1. **"renders three column regions"** — `expect(screen.getAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(3)` 2. **"renders Ready column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` 3. **"renders In Progress column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'In Progress' })` 4. **"renders Done column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Done' })` 5. **"shows loading indicator when loading is true"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })` then render; assert `screen.getByLabelText('Loading')` is in the document AND `expect(screen.queryAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(0)` (no columns rendered) 6. **"shows error banner when error is set"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })` then render; assert `screen.getByRole('alert')` contains text `'fetch failed'` 7. **"passes ready cards to Ready column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Ready' })).getByText('Test Card')` — verifies card is inside the Ready column specifically 8. **"passes in_progress cards to In Progress column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c2', column: 'in-progress' }], done: [] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'In Progress' })).getByText('Test Card')` 9. **"passes done cards to Done column"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c3', column: 'done' }] } })` then render; assert `within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Done' })).getByText('Test Card')` 10. **"opens modal when a card is clicked"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`; verify `screen.getByRole('dialog')` appears 11. **"closes modal when close button is clicked"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] } })` then render; open modal by `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))`; then `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))`; verify `screen.queryByRole('dialog')` is null #### 1b. Implement `Board.jsx` ```jsx import { useState } from 'react' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Column from './Column.jsx' import CardModal from './CardModal.jsx' import './Board.css' export default function Board() { const { cards, loading, error } = useBoard() const [selectedCard, setSelectedCard] = useState(null) if (loading) return

Write failing tests: `Column.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest' import Column from './Column.jsx' const makeCard = (id, title, pos) => ({ id, title, assignee: 'Alice', description: 'desc', column: 'ready', position: pos, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], }) const MOCK_CARDS = [makeCard('1', 'Card One', 1), makeCard('2', 'Card Two', 2), makeCard('3', 'Card Three', 3)] ``` Test cases — render with ``: 1. **"renders the column title"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` 2. **"renders card count equal to number of cards"** — badge span has text content `'3'`; query with `screen.getByLabelText('3 cards')` (the `aria-label` on the count span) 3. **"renders 0 count for empty column"** — render with `cards={[]}`, `screen.getByLabelText('0 cards')` 4. **"renders a CardTile for each card"** — `screen.getByText('Card One')`, `screen.getByText('Card Two')`, `screen.getByText('Card Three')` all present 5. **"renders empty state message when no cards"** — render with `cards={[]}`, `screen.getByText(/no cards/i)` 6. **"calls onCardClick with the correct card when a CardTile is clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, click `screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Card One' })`, verify `handler` called with `MOCK_CARDS[0]` #### 2b. Write failing tests: `CardTile.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest' import CardTile from './CardTile.jsx' const card = { id: '1', title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'Short desc', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } ``` All tests render with `render()` unless noted. Test cases: 1. **"has dialog role"** — `screen.getByRole('dialog')` is in the document 2. **"renders card title inside dialog"** — `screen.getByRole('dialog')` contains text `'Fix bug'` 3. **"renders assignee name"** — dialog contains text `'Alice'` 4. **"renders 'Unassigned' when assignee is null"** — render with `{ ...card, assignee: null }`, dialog contains `'Unassigned'` 5. **"renders description when present"** — dialog contains `'Some description'` 6. **"renders 'No description' when description is null"** — render with `{ ...card, description: null }`, dialog contains `'No description'` 7. **"calls onClose when close button is clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, render with `onClose={handler}`, `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))`, verify `handler` called once 8. **"calls onClose when Escape key is pressed"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, render with `onClose={handler}`, `fireEvent.keyDown(document.body, { key: 'Escape' })`, verify `handler` called once 9. **"calls onClose when modal overlay is clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, render with `onClose={handler}`, `fireEvent.click(document.querySelector('.modal-overlay'))`, verify `handler` called once 10. **"does not call onClose when modal content area is clicked"** — `const handler = vi.fn()`, render with `onClose={handler}`, `fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('dialog'))`, verify `handler` NOT called #### 4b. Implement `CardModal.jsx` ```jsx import { useEffect } from 'react' import './CardModal.css' export default function CardModal({ card, onClose }) { useEffect(() => { function onKey(e) { if (e.key === 'Escape') onClose() } document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) }, [onClose]) return (
e.stopPropagation()}>

) } ``` #### 4c. `CardModal.css` ```css .modal-overlay { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0,0,0,.5); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; z-index: 100; } .modal-content { background: #fff; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.5rem; max-width: 480px; width: 90%; position: relative; box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,.2); } .modal-close { position: absolute; top: 0.75rem; right: 0.75rem; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.1rem; color: #666; } .modal-close:hover { color: #1a1a2e; } .modal-title { font-size: 1.2rem; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; color: #1a1a2e; } .modal-assignee { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #555; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; } .modal-description { font-size: 0.875rem; color: #333; line-height: 1.5; } ``` --- ### App.jsx Update Modify `client/src/App.jsx`: ```jsx import Board from './components/Board/Board.jsx' import './App.css' function App() { return (

Kanban Board

) } export default App ``` Modify `client/src/App.test.jsx` — add `vi.mock` for `useBoard` at the top (before imports) since Board now renders inside App and would otherwise trigger real API calls. Preserve all 4 existing tests unchanged: ```js import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, vi } from 'vitest' import App from './App.jsx' vi.mock('./hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn(() => ({ cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), })) })) // ... existing 4 test cases remain unchanged ``` Note: `vi.mock` is hoisted by Vitest automatically, so placement in the file is safe. The mock path `'./hooks/useBoard.js'` is relative to `App.test.jsx` (`client/src/`) and resolves to the same module that Board.jsx imports via `'../../hooks/useBoard.js'` — Vitest deduplicates by resolved path. --- ## TDD Execution Order (per subtask) For each subtask: 1. **Red**: Create the `*.test.jsx` file, run `npm test -- --run` → confirm failures 2. **Green**: Create the `*.jsx` and `*.css` files → run tests → confirm pass 3. **Refactor**: Clean up while keeping tests green Run tests from `client/` directory: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test -- --run ``` Or watch mode during development: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test ``` --- ## Verification Checklist - [ ] All new test files pass: Board, Column, CardTile, CardModal - [ ] Existing tests still pass: App, useBoard, api/client - [ ] `npm run build` completes without errors - [ ] Dev server (`npm run dev`) shows three-column board at localhost:5173 - [ ] Cards from the API appear in the correct column - [ ] Clicking a card opens the modal with title, assignee, description - [ ] Pressing Escape or clicking overlay closes the modal - [ ] Responsive: columns stack on narrow screens - [ ] Keyboard navigation: Tab → card, Enter/Space → opens modal

content.thinking:
Let me carefully re-evaluate the plan one more time for any remaining issues.

### Issue 1: Board test 1 - `screen.getAllByRole('region')` — `<section>` with `aria-label` is a region, but `<header>` inside the column also has a header role

Wait, actually the Column.jsx renders:
```jsx
<section className="column" aria-label={title}>
  <header className="column-header">
    ...
  </header>
  ...
</section>
```

The `<header>` inside a `<section>` has the role `banner` when it's a top-level header, but when it's inside a sectioning element like `<section>`, it has the role `generic` (not `banner` and not `header` landmark). So `<header>` inside `<section>` does NOT appear as a landmark. 


So `getAllByRole('region')` should only find the 3 `<section>` elements, not the `<header>` elements. This is fine.

### Issue 2: `DEFAULT_STATE` — `vi.fn()` at module level vs in `beforeEach`

The `DEFAULT_STATE` is defined at module level with `vi.fn()` calls, which causes problems when tests share the same mock instances. I need to move the mock function creation into `beforeEach` so each test gets fresh mocks.

Actually, wait — `Board.jsx` only uses `{ cards, loading, error }` from `useBoard()`, so it never calls `createCard`, `updateCard`, or any other mocked functions. The call counts don't matter here. And since tests use spread syntax when overriding state properties, they won't mutate the shared `DEFAULT_STATE` object. This is fine.

### Issue 3: `screen.getAllByRole('region')` with `toHaveLength(3)`

For Board test 1, `DEFAULT_STATE` has empty cards, so each Column renders with a "No cards" message. The 3 `<section>` elements with `aria-label` become region landmarks, giving exactly 3 regions. That's correct.

### Issue 4: Board test 2 - `getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })`

The Column has both an `<h2>` with text "Ready" and a `<section>` with `aria-label="Ready"`. But `getByRole('heading', ...)` only matches heading elements, not sections, so it correctly targets the `<h2>` and ignores the section's accessible name.

### Issue 5: CardModal test 9 - `document.querySelector('.modal-overlay')`

The CardModal renders a div with class `modal-overlay` that covers the viewport and has an `onClick` handler to close the modal. When the test clicks this overlay, it should trigger the close action.

The event flow is straightforward here — clicking the overlay directly fires its `onClick` handler, and since the modal content has `stopPropagation()`, it won't interfere with clicks on the overlay itself. This should work correctly.

For the dialog element test, clicking the `.modal-content` div triggers its `stopPropagation()` handler, which prevents the event from bubbling up to the overlay's `onClose`. The close button inside has its own `onClick={onClose}`, but since we're clicking the content area itself, that won't fire either.

For the CardTile test, I need to verify that when description is null, the description element doesn't render by checking that the text 'Short desc' isn't in the DOM.

Now looking at the Column test with duplicate card titles — since we're rendering with mock data that includes multiple cards with the same title, `screen.getByText('Card One')` will throw an error because it can't determine which element to return.

For the button role query, the CardTile component wraps everything in a div with `role="button"` and sets `aria-label` to the card title, so `screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Card One' })` should work correctly since it matches against the aria-label.

Now looking at the heading query in the Column test — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` will only find actual heading elements like the `<h2>`, not the section's aria-label, so I need to check what the h2's accessible name actually is.

For CardModal's event listener cleanup, the useEffect properly removes the keydown listener when the component unmounts, and since testing-library runs cleanup after each test by default, the listener gets removed. When testing the Escape key, the event fired on document.body bubbles up to document where the listener is attached, so that works correctly.

Now checking the Board test 1 to see what it's rendering...

the test expects 3 region roles, and since Board renders 3 Column components with `<section aria-label={title}>` elements when loading is false and there's no error, those sections do have the region role because they have accessible names. So the assertion should pass.

For tests 1-4, I need to verify whether the beforeEach is actually rendering Board or if that's missing from the setup. I need to make sure tests 1-4 explicitly call `render(<Board />)` before their assertions, since the setup only mocks the state but doesn't render the component. For tests 5-11, the plan already specifies the mock setup followed by render, so those are clear. Now I'm noticing that `fireEvent` is imported in the Column test but only used in one test case, which isn't a correctness issue. Looking at the Board tests, `DEFAULT_STATE` has `vi.fn()` instances defined outside `beforeEach`, so if mocks get reset during test runs, those function mocks could be affected—though the primitive values and objects in the state wouldn't be impacted since the component only uses certain parts of it. I'm realizing the cleanest approach is to put `render(<Board />)` in `beforeEach` for tests 1-4, then have tests 5-11 set up their mock state and call render individually—each render creates a fresh component instance that testing-library handles correctly.

Now I'm checking the `DEFAULT_STATE` object defined at module scope with `vi.fn()` calls—these mock functions are created once when the module loads, so every test gets the same mock function references, which could cause state pollution if `beforeEach` reuses them without resetting.

But since `Board.jsx` only destructures `cards`, `loading`, and `error` and never actually calls those mutation functions, it's not a practical issue here. The functions are just there to match the real `useBoard()` shape.

The actual problem I need to fix is that tests 1-4 don't explicitly call `render(<Board />)` in the test body itself. And I'm also noticing the CardTile test 6 passes a `card` object with `created_at: Date.now()`, so I need to verify that when `onCardClick` is invoked, it's receiving the exact same card reference.

Since the test passes the same object reference and Vitest uses deep equality for `toHaveBeenCalledWith`, this should work fine. Now I need to address the render call issue—I could put it in `beforeEach` for the common case, but then tests 5-11 would need their own render calls, which risks rendering multiple components in the DOM if I'm not careful about cleanup.

The cleaner approach is to make the render call explicit in each test rather than hiding it in `beforeEach`. For tests 1-4, I'll add the render call directly before the assertions, and tests 5-11 already specify when to render after setting up their mocks. Let me update tests 1-4 to be clear about this.

content.text: I found one remaining correctness issue: **Board tests 1–4 never say to call `render(<Board />)`**. The `beforeEach` only sets the mock return value — there is no render there — so tests 1–4 have no instruction to actually mount the component before asserting. An LLM would either fail with "unable to find element" or arbitrarily add a render in `beforeEach` that would conflict with tests 5–11 (which need custom mock state before rendering). The render call needs to be explicit in every test.

# Plan: Create Board, Column, and CardTile Components (Task 10) ## Context The Kanban board app has a working API client (`client/src/api/client.js`) and a `useBoard` custom hook (`client/src/hooks/useBoard.js`) that manages grouped card state. `App.jsx` is a placeholder with a comment where the Board should go. No UI components exist yet. This task builds the visual layer: Board → Column → CardTile, plus a basic CardModal activated by clicking a card. --- ## Architecture Decisions - **Plain CSS files** (e.g. `Board.css`) — consistent with `App.css`; no CSS modules or styled-components - **Flexbox** for the three-column layout (no CSS Grid needed here) - **`useBoard()` called in `Board.jsx`** — column/card data flows down as props; Column and CardTile are pure presentational - **`selectedCard` state in `Board.jsx`** — drives modal visibility; passed down via `onCardClick` prop chain - **All component files co-located** under `client/src/components/Board/` - **Testing**: Vitest + @testing-library/react, matching existing patterns; `vi.mock` to isolate `useBoard` --- ## Files to Create | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` | Board tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` | Board container | | `client/src/components/Board/Board.css` | Board styles | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` | Column tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` | Column component | | `client/src/components/Board/Column.css` | Column styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx` | CardTile tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx` | CardTile component | | `client/src/components/Board/CardTile.css` | CardTile styles | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | CardModal tests (write first) | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Card detail modal | | `client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css` | Modal overlay styles | ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/App.jsx` | Import and render `` in `
` | | `client/src/App.test.jsx` | Add `vi.mock` for `useBoard` (Board now renders inside App) | --- ## Reusable Existing Code - **`useBoard`** at `client/src/hooks/useBoard.js` — provides `{ cards, loading, error }` where `cards = { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }` - **`columnToKey`** exported from `useBoard.js` — maps `'in-progress'` → `'in_progress'` - **Card shape**: `{ id, title, assignee: string|null, description: string|null, column, position, created_at, comments[] }` - **Test mock pattern** from `useBoard.test.js`: `vi.mock('../api/client.js', () => ({ fetchCards: vi.fn() }))` and `mockResponse()` helper --- ## TDD Implementation Plan ### Subtask 1 — Board Layout Component #### 1a. Write failing tests: `Board.test.jsx` ```js import { render, screen, fireEvent, within } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Board from './Board.jsx' vi.mock('../../hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn() })) const MOCK_CARD = { id: 'c1', title: 'Test Card', assignee: 'Alice', description: 'A description', column: 'ready', position: 1, created_at: Date.now(), comments: [], } const DEFAULT_STATE = { cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), } beforeEach(() => { useBoard.mockReturnValue(DEFAULT_STATE) }) ``` Test cases: 1. **"renders three column regions"** — `expect(screen.getAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(3)` 2. **"renders Ready column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })` 3. **"renders In Progress column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'In Progress' })` 4. **"renders Done column heading"** — `screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Done' })` 5. **"shows loading indicator when loading is true"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })` then render; assert `screen.getByLabelText('Loading')` is in the document AND `expect(screen.queryAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(0)` (no columns rendered) 6. **"shows error banner when error is set"** — call `useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })` then render; The mock path `'./hooks/useBoard.js'` is relative to `App.test.jsx` (`client/src/`) and resolves to the same module that Board.jsx imports via `'../../hooks/useBoard.js'` — Vitest deduplicates by resolved path. --- ## TDD Execution Order (per subtask) For each subtask: 1. **Red**: Create the `*.test.jsx` file, run `npm test -- --run` → confirm failures 2. **Green**: Create the `*.jsx` and `*.css` files → run tests → confirm pass 3. **Refactor**: Clean up while keeping tests green Run tests from `client/` directory: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test -- --run ``` Or watch mode during development: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test ``` --- ## Verification Checklist - [ ] All new test files pass: Board, Column, CardTile, CardModal - [ ] Existing tests still pass: App, useBoard, api/client - [ ] `npm run build` completes without errors - [ ] Dev server (`npm run dev`) shows three-column board at localhost:5173 - [ ] Cards from the API appear in the correct column - [ ] Clicking a card opens the modal with title, assignee, description - [ ] Pressing Escape or clicking overlay closes the modal - [ ] Responsive: columns stack on narrow screens - [ ] Keyboard navigation: Tab → card, Enter/Space → opens modal

Tests 5–11 already had explicit render instructions. This prevents an LLM from either forgetting to render at all, or incorrectly adding a render to `beforeEach` that would conflict with the per-test custom mock setups in tests 5–11.

Preserve all 4 existing tests unchanged: ```js import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, vi } from 'vitest' import App from './App.jsx' vi.mock('./hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn(() => ({ cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }, loading: false, error: null, createCard: vi.fn(), updateCard: vi.fn(), deleteCard: vi.fn(), moveCard: vi.fn(), addComment: vi.fn(), })) })) // ... existing 4 test cases remain unchanged ``` Note: `vi.mock` is hoisted by Vitest automatically, so placement in the file is safe. The mock path `'./hooks/useBoard.js'` is relative to `App.test.jsx` (`client/src/`) and resolves to the same module that Board.jsx imports via `'../../hooks/useBoard.js'` — Vitest deduplicates by resolved path. --- ## TDD Execution Order (per subtask) For each subtask: 1. **Red**: Create the `*.test.jsx` file, run `npm test -- --run` → confirm failures 2. **Green**: Create the `*.jsx` and `*.css` files → run tests → confirm pass 3. **Refactor**: Clean up while keeping tests green Run tests from `client/` directory: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test -- --run ``` Or watch mode during development: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test ``` --- ## Verification Checklist - [ ] All new test files pass: Board, Column, CardTile, CardModal - [ ] Existing tests still pass: App, useBoard, api/client - [ ] `npm run build` completes without errors - [ ] Dev server (`npm run dev`) shows three-column board at localhost:5173 - [ ] Cards from the API appear in the correct column - [ ] Clicking a card opens the modal with title, assignee, description - [ ] Pressing Escape or clicking overlay closes the modal - [ ] Responsive: columns stack on narrow screens - [ ] Keyboard navigation: Tab → card, Enter/Space → opens modal
